channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: Swedish tax authority freezes Kubal’s funds as controlled by Deripaska
item.description: Skatteverket, the Swedish tax authority, has issued an administrative decision (not yet published) finding that EU-sanctioned oligarch Oleg Deripaska indirectly controls aluminium plant Kubal and ther

item.title: France releases ‘MT Tagor’ tanker after imposing €1m fine
item.description: Oil tanker ‘MT Tagor’ (IMO 9282481) has been released from French custody, after the Maritime Court in Brest accepted a plea deal with the vessel’s owners. They have agreed to pay a fine of €1 million

item.title: Finnish CEO sentenced for breaching Russia sanctions and company to forfeit benefits
item.description: The CEO (Risto Riihimäki) of Finnish company Idän liikkenvälitys IL Oy has been sentenced to 3 years and 8 months imprisonment for using the company to perpetrate the export of equipment to Russia in

item.title: 2 German Russian nationals plead guilty to breaching Russia sanctions
item.description: 2 German Russian dual nationals have pleaded guilty in the Münster Regional Court to breaching Germany's Foreign Trade and Payments Act by exporting industrial equipment to Russia in violation of EU s

item.title: European Court of Justice interprets ‘operator’ in Russia Reg 833 broadcast prohibition & disagrees with European Commission’s FAQs
item.description: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has given a preliminary ruling in Case C-67/25 R, N & K v Staatsanwaltschaft Saarbrücken, in a reference for a preliminary ruling from the Saarbrü

item.title: US terrorism victims bring claim against BAT related to North Korea sanctions breaches
item.description: American victims of terrorist attacks in Iraq have filed a complaint against British American Tobacco (BAT) claiming that BAT aided and abetted the attacks by “knowingly providing substantial assistan

item.title: US extends licences related to sale of Serbian refinery to 31 July 2026
item.description: The US has extended the temporary licence on Serbia’s Russian-owned oil refinery Naftna Industrija Srbije (NIS) until 31 July 2026, according to the Serbian energy minister. The licence had been exten

item.title: OFAC reminder to file 2026 blocked property report by 30 September 2026
item.description: The US Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has published a reminder that holders of property blocked under OFAC regulations as of 30 June 2026 must provide OFAC with a comprehensive list of all su

item.title: US designates Ecuadorean gang as a terrorist organisation
item.description: The US has designated the ‘Chone Killers’ as a foreign terrorist organisation (FTO) and as a specially designated global terrorist (SDGT) – press release. The ‘Chone Killers’ is an Ecuadorean organisa

item.title: US adds 6 Primeiro Comando da Capital linked people & entities to sanctions list
item.description: The US has added 2 people and 4 entities linked to Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC) to its counterterrorism sanctions list – press release. The US proscribed Brazil-based PCC as a terrorist organi
